message:
feat(layout): Expand responsive layout for ultrawide and 4K monitors

Removes artificial max-width constraints and adds progressive breakpoints
to fully utilize available screen real estate on large displays.

styles.css changes:
- .app-body max-width increased from 1280px to 1400px base
- 1600px+: max-width 1600px with larger padding
- 1920px+: max-width calc(100% - 6rem) fills most of screen
- 2560px+: max-width calc(100% - 8rem) maximizes 4K utilization
- .detail-viewers grid gets proportional columns and scaling gaps
  - 1440px+: 1.2fr/1fr (sequence gets more space)
  - 1920px+: 1.1fr/1fr with generous spacing
  - 2560px+: balanced 1fr/1fr with maximum gaps
- Import for new desktop-ui.css stylesheet

components.css changes:
- .two-column grid enhanced with responsive breakpoints
- 1440px+: minmax(320px, 420px) for list column
- 1920px+: minmax(360px, 500px) with larger gaps
- 2560px+: minmax(400px, 600px) maximizing space
- 3440px+ ultrawide: fixed list at 500px with massive gaps
- .list max-height scales from 50vh to 75vh on ultrawides
- .panel padding increases progressively on larger screens
- .viewer-panel gets min-height scaling for 3D views

index.css changes:
- .three-container heights now use clamp() for responsive scaling
- 1440px+: clamp(400px, 55vh, 700px)
- 1920px+: clamp(450px, 60vh, 800px)
- 2560px+: clamp(500px, 65vh, 900px)
- 3840px+ (4K): up to 80vh container height

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
